task: I have a short school news piece to put together from a few notes and quotes, but some of the details are still a little mixed together. Can you help me figure out a clean way to organize what I have before I write it up?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — I can help you organize it into a clear news structure before you write.

Send me:
- your notes
- any quotes you want to include
- the topic/event
- any details you’re unsure about

Then I can help you turn it into:
1. a clean outline,
2. a suggested lead sentence,
3. a logical order for the facts and quotes,
4. and, if you want, a full draft in news style.

If it helps, you can paste everything as-is, even if it’s messy.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah, but I need to actually see the notes first. It’s for the school newspaper, and I don’t want to guess at the facts.

Here’s the messy version:
- Topic: the cafeteria had to shut down for part of the morning because of a water leak near the kitchen
- It happened before first period, but I’m not 100% sure on the exact time yet
- Students were told to go to the auditorium while staff cleaned it up
- Quote from a custodian: “We had to turn off the water and make sure the floor was safe before anyone could come through.”
- Quote from a student: “I thought it was going to be a huge mess, but it was mostly just confusing.”
- Quote from an assistant principal: “Lunch service should be back to normal after the repairs are finished.”
- I also have one note that some classes started late, but I don’t know if that’s because of this or something else

I want the order to be clean and not dramatic. Just straight facts.

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — here’s a clean, straight-news way to organize it without guessing or adding drama.

## 1. What you know for sure
- The cafeteria shut down for part of the morning because of a water leak near the kitchen.
- Students were sent to the auditorium while staff cleaned up.
- A custodian said the water had to be turned off and the floor made safe.
- A student said the situation was confusing, but not as bad as expected.
- An assistant principal said lunch service should return to normal after repairs are finished.

## 2. What still needs confirmation
- The exact time the leak happened.
- Whether the late-started classes were related to the leak or something else.

## 3. Best order for the story
A simple news order would be:

1. **Lead:** cafeteria closed due to water leak near kitchen
2. **What happened next:** students moved to auditorium
3. **Why it happened / safety response:** custodian quote
4. **Student reaction:** student quote
5. **Status update:** assistant principal quote about lunch service
6. **Unclear detail:** mention late classes only if you confirm they were related

## 4. Suggested lead sentence
Here are two clean options:

- **A water leak near the kitchen forced the cafeteria to close for part of the morning, sending students to the auditorium while staff cleaned up the area.**
- **The cafeteria was closed for part of the morning after a water leak near the kitchen, prompting staff to move students to the auditorium during cleanup.**

## 5. Simple outline
**Paragraph 1:** What happened and where  
**Paragraph 2:** What students were told to do  
**Paragraph 3:** Quote from custodian explaining the safety steps  
**Paragraph 4:** Quote from student reaction  
**Paragraph 5:** Quote from assistant principal about lunch service  
**Paragraph 6:** Optional note about class delays, only if confirmed

## 6. A clean draft structure
If you want, you could write it like this:

> A water leak near the kitchen forced the cafeteria to close for part of the morning, school staff said. Students were directed to the auditorium while custodians cleaned up the area and turned off the water.  
>
> “We had to turn off the water and make sure the floor was safe before anyone could come through,” said a custodian.  
>
> Some students said the situation was more confusing than serious. “I thought it was going to be a huge mess, but it was mostly just confusing,” one student said.  
>
> An assistant principal said lunch service should return to normal after repairs are finished.

## 7. Best next step
If you want, I can also help you turn this into:
- a **finished 4–5 paragraph school news article**, or
- a **more polished headline + lead + body** version.

If you paste the exact wording you want to use, I can help tighten it up.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, this is good. Clean order, nothing extra, and you didn’t make up the late classes part. The draft structure works. I can write from that.
